input: Audit this React e-commerce product page for WCAG 2.2 AA compliance and provide a prioritized remediation plan.
criteria: References the 4-phase audit methodology (automated scan, keyboard navigation, screen reader testing, expert manual review) and explains why automated tools alone catch only ~30% of issues
Tests keyboard navigation through the full purchase flow (product selection, add to cart, checkout) and verifies visible focus indicators, tab order, and skip links
Performs screen reader verification of product images (alt text), prices, form labels, error messages, and dynamic cart updates using aria-live regions
References specific WCAG 2.2 AA criteria by number (e.g., 1.1.1, 1.4.3, 2.1.1, 2.4.7, 4.1.2) for each finding, not just generic descriptions
Produces a prioritized remediation plan with severity classification (critical/major/minor), effort estimates, and a risk-vs-effort ordering that addresses blocking issues first

input: Build an accessible autocomplete/combobox component that works with screen readers.
criteria: Uses correct ARIA roles (combobox on input, listbox on suggestions container, option on each suggestion) with aria-expanded, aria-autocomplete, aria-controls, and aria-activedescendant
Implements complete keyboard patterns: Down/Up Arrow to navigate suggestions, Enter to select, Escape to close, typing to filter, Home/End for first/last option
Includes live region announcement of results count (e.g., '5 results available') and 'No results found' state, with the live region container pre-existing in the DOM
Manages focus correctly: keeps DOM focus on the input while using aria-activedescendant to communicate the highlighted option to screen readers
Addresses screen reader rendering differences (JAWS forms mode, VoiceOver aria-activedescendant requirements, NVDA browse mode behavior) and tests with at least two combinations

input: Our company received a demand letter about ADA web accessibility. What do we need to do?
criteria: Recommends creating a VPAT/ACR with proper conformance levels (Supports, Partially Supports, Does Not Support, Not Applicable) and specifies which assistive technologies to test with
Outlines a realistic remediation timeline with severity-based SLAs (P0 critical within 2 weeks, P1 major within 30 days) and emphasizes 'substantial compliance' as the legal standard

Explains legal defensibility requirements: documented remediation effort, accessibility statement with contact info, conformance roadmap, regular re-auditing schedule, and accessible alternative pathways
Identifies immediate high-impact fixes (keyboard access, form labels, alt text, color contrast, page language) that demonstrate good faith effort with minimal development time
Warns against accessibility overlays (AccessiBe, UserWay) as a remediation strategy, citing NFB opposition and their documented failure in ADA lawsuits, and recommends source code fixes instead

input: Convert this div-based navigation menu to be fully accessible.
criteria: Replaces div structure with semantic HTML (nav element with aria-label, ul/li list structure, a elements for links) rather than applying ARIA roles to divs
Implements keyboard support with arrow keys for moving within the menu, Tab for moving between top-level items or out of the menu, Escape to close submenus, and Enter/Space to activate items
Applies correct ARIA attributes for any non-native behavior (aria-expanded on items with submenus, aria-haspopup for dropdown triggers, aria-current='page' for current page indicator)
Handles focus management for submenus (focus moves into submenu on open, returns to parent on close, roving tabindex or aria-activedescendant for item navigation)

Addresses mobile touch support with appropriate touch target sizes (minimum 44x44 CSS pixels per platform guidelines) and ensures the menu works with mobile screen readers (VoiceOver gestures, TalkBack)

input: Set up automated accessibility testing in our CI/CD pipeline.
criteria: Recommends axe-core as the primary engine with specific configuration: tag filters (wcag2a, wcag2aa), exclusion patterns for third-party content, and threshold settings (zero violations for new projects, baseline regression for legacy)

Configures rules to handle false positives: exclusion list with documented justifications, custom rule overrides for known edge cases, and separation of gate-blocking (critical/major) from warning-only (minor) findings
Includes testing of dynamic content by running axe after user interactions (page load alone misses SPA route changes, lazy-loaded content, and AJAX updates)
Specifies reporting format with WCAG criterion reference, severity, affected element selector, and remediation guidance -- not just pass/fail

Addresses integration with the broader testing strategy: axe-core catches ~30% of issues, CI gates provide regression prevention, but manual keyboard and screen reader testing are still required on a scheduled cadence
